title: bromus macrostachys subsp. danthoniae
intro: Bromus macrostachys subsp. danthoniae, also known as Danthonia brome, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Danthonia brome include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Danthonia brome can be prepared as a tea by infusing the dried leaves and flowers in hot water or as a tincture by mixing the plant extract with a solvent like ethanol.
